title: Why France's national weather service struggles to accurately predict heatwaves
subhead: Days ahead of the heatwaves of June and July, Météo-France slightly underestimated their intensity and duration. The reason has to do with limits in forecasting models for extreme events, as well as the challenge of explaining the uncertainty of weather predictions to the general public.

US targets ICC in new offensive
subhead: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio reopened a diplomatic campaign on Monday aimed at dismantling the ICC by combining sanctions, putting pressure on member states and seeking allies among major powers hostile to the institution.

title: China's foreign trade surges, driven by AI and electric vehicles
subhead: China's exports rose by 27% in June compared to the same month last year. Despite an increase in imports, the country posted a trade surplus of $576 billion in the first half of the year.

title: Spain stun France to reach World Cup 2026 final
subhead: France had entered the showdown as the overwhelming favorites for the tournament, but failed to score in the 2-0 match on Tuesday. Spain will now face either Argentina or England in Sunday's final in New Jersey.

title: What does France's assisted dying bill contain, as MPs prepare to cast their final vote?
subhead: As it reaches the end of its parliamentary journey with Wednesday's vote in the  Assemblée Nationale, the proposed law would establish the right to assisted suicide or euthanasia for patients experiencing constant suffering from serious and incurable illnesses, under strictly regulated conditions.

title: Is air conditioning good or bad? Join this (fake) group chat for (real) analysis
subhead: Each time a new heatwave hits, the question of air conditioning and its environmental impacts crops up in the public debate. Here are the key points to help you understand what is at stake.

title: 'If they catch me, it's over for me': Senegal's LGBTQ+ community lives in daily fear of repression
subhead: In March, Senegal passed a law increasing penalties for same-sex relations, leaving LGBTQ+ people living in fear. Several described their deeply uncertain daily lives, gripped by the constant anxiety of being arrested.
